questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A is three times as old as B, and the sum of their ages is $48$ years. A's age is _____ years.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Express both ages in one variable.

**Working.** $A=3B$ and $A+B=48\Rightarrow 4B=48\Rightarrow B=12,\;A=36$.

**Answer.** $36$ years.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: What is $35\%$ of $240$?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Percent means parts per hundred.

**Working.**
$$\frac{35}{100}\times 240 = 84$$

**Answer.** $84$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct comparative form: 'This seam is ____ than the one we mined last year.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** One-syllable adjectives form the comparative with '-er', and 'than' signals a comparative (not superlative).

**Working.** 'Thick' becomes 'thicker'; 'more thicker' is a double comparative and 'thickest' is superlative.

**Answer.** thicker.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A can finish a job in $12$ days and B in $6$ days. Working together, they finish it in _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add the per-day work rates.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{12}+\dfrac{1}{6}=\dfrac{1}{12}+\dfrac{2}{12}=\dfrac{3}{12}=\dfrac{1}{4}$, so $4$ days.

**Answer.** $4$ days.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the area of the triangle shown with base $6$ and height $4$.

figure.caption: Triangle base and height
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,120 160,120 100,40' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='100' y1='120' x2='100' y2='40' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='90' y='135' font-size='8'>base 6</text><text x='105' y='85' font-size='8'>height 4</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Area $=\tfrac{1}{2}bh$.

**Working.**
$$A=\tfrac{1}{2}(6)(4)=12$$

**Answer.** $12$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A person faces north, turns $90^\circ$ clockwise, then turns $180^\circ$. Which direction is the person now facing?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Track each turn from the current facing.

**Working.** North → ($90^\circ$ CW) East → ($180^\circ$) West.

**Answer.** West.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: THERMOMETER : TEMPERATURE :: BAROMETER : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'instrument : quantity it measures'.

**Working.** A thermometer measures temperature; a barometer measures atmospheric pressure.

**Answer.** pressure.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A monthly budget of $3600$ is split as Labour $25\%$, Material $40\%$, Equipment $20\%$ and Other $15\%$ (see pie chart). The amount spent on Labour is

figure.caption: Pie chart of a 3600 budget: Labour 25%, Material 40%, Equipment 20%, Other 15%.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 230 190' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><circle cx='110' cy='90' r='70' fill='none' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='110' y1='90' x2='180' y2='90' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='110' y1='90' x2='110' y2='20' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='110' y1='90' x2='69' y2='147' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='110' y1='90' x2='151' y2='147' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='1'/><text x='130' y='60' font-size='10' fill='#6366f1'>Labour 25%</text><text x='40' y='70' font-size='10' fill='#10b981'>Material 40%</text><text x='70' y='165' font-size='10' fill='#f59e0b'>Equip 20%</text><text x='150' y='130' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>Other 15%</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sector amount = percentage of the total.

**Working.** $25\%$ of $3600 = 0.25\times 3600 = 900$.

**Answer.** $900$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'New safety measures were introduced to ____ the risk of roof collapse.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Safety measures aim to reduce risk.

**Working.** 'Mitigate' means to make less severe; 'aggravate' would worsen the risk.

**Answer.** mitigate.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: How many rectangles (of all sizes) are there in a strip of three unit cells in a row (shown)?

figure.caption: A horizontal strip divided into three equal unit cells.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='15' y='20' width='150' height='40' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='65' y1='20' x2='65' y2='60' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='115' y1='20' x2='115' y2='60' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count rectangles spanning $1$, $2$ or $3$ cells.

**Working.** Single cells $=3$, pairs $=2$, the full strip $=1$; total $=3+2+1=6$.

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A diesel-contaminated soil is cleaned in place by injecting air and nutrients to stimulate indigenous hydrocarbon-degrading bacteria, with no excavation. This technique is best classified as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Treating soil where it lies by stimulating native microbes with O₂/nutrients is in-situ bioremediation (bioventing); ex-situ methods require excavation, and incineration/S-S are not biological.

**Working.**
No excavation + stimulate native microbes ⇒ in-situ.

**Answer.** In-situ bioremediation

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A higher value of the Shannon–Wiener index $H'$ indicates
questions.solution:
**Concept.** H' increases with richness and evenness.

**Working.**
Greater diversity.

**Answer.** Greater diversity

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: For the settling tank shown, read the flow $Q$ (MLD) and plan area $A$ from the diagram. The surface overflow rate $Q/A$ is _____ m³/m²/day.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="40" width="250" height="90" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M20 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="14" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">Q = 6 MLD</text><path d="M290 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="300" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">out</text><line x1="40" y1="48" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="120" cy="80" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="170" cy="100" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="210" cy="118" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><path d="M120 86 L132 124 M170 106 L182 126" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="0.8"/><text x="120" y="150" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">plan area A = 200 m²</text><text x="160" y="36" font-size="8" fill="#2563eb">surface (overflow)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Flow Q and plan area A marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** SOR = Q/A, with Q and A read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{6\times1000}{200}=30.$$

**Answer.** $30$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $3\,\text{t}$ of a greenhouse gas. Reading the gas's GWP₁₀₀ from the figure, its CO₂-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="36" width="220" height="56" rx="6" fill="rgba(217,119,6,0.12)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">the gas</text><text x="150" y="80" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">GWP₁₀₀ = 28</text><text x="150" y="112" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂-e = mass × GWP</text></svg>
figure.caption: Global-warming potential of the gas marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$3\times28=84.$$

**Answer.** $84$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: Read the present population and the ecological carrying capacity from the bar chart. The carrying-capacity utilisation is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="97.2" width="50" height="52.8" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="93.2" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">44000</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">population</text><rect x="180" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">100000</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">carrying capacity</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">persons</text></svg>
figure.caption: Present population and carrying capacity shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Utilisation = population/carrying capacity ×100, both read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{44000}{100000}\times100=44\%.$$

**Answer.** $44\%$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A solution has pOH $=2.5$ at 25 °C. Its pH is _____.

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: GPP $2000$, respiration $600\,\text{g C/m}^2/\text{yr}$. NPP is _____ g C/m²/yr.

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $1000\,\text{kg}$ at $60\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$1000(1-60/100)=400.$$

**Answer.** $400\,\text{kg}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: Which species are associated with photochemical (Los-Angeles type) smog?
options: Sulphur dioxide as the chief agent
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Photochemical smog is an oxidising NOₓ/VOC–sunlight system producing O₃ and PAN; SO₂ characterises reducing/London smog.

**Working.**
O₃, PAN, NOₓ.

**Answer.** Ozone, PAN and NOₓ

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: A tannery's discharge requires $₹4.5$ lakh of effluent-treatment (abatement) and additionally causes $₹2.8$ lakh of measurable downstream crop damage. Under the polluter-pays principle, the amount (in $₹$ lakh) the tannery must internalise is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The polluter-pays principle internalises the full externality: the polluter bears abatement **plus** damage costs (not just one of them).

**Working.**
$$4.5+2.8=7.3\text{ lakh}.$$

**Answer.** $₹7.3$ lakh

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: Atmospheric CO₂ is $410\,\text{ppm}$ rising at $2\,\text{ppm/yr}$. After $9$ years it is _____ ppm.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Linear extrapolation: C = C₀ + rate×t.

**Working.**
$$410+2\times9=428.$$

**Answer.** $428\,\text{ppm}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Energy recovery by incineration ranks, in the waste hierarchy, above only
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Order: reduce>reuse>recycle>recover>dispose. Recovery sits just above landfill.

**Working.**
Above landfill disposal.

**Answer.** Landfill disposal

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: City of $2,00,000$ at $0.6\,\text{kg/cap/day}$. Daily MSW is _____ t/day.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total = pop × rate.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{200000\times0.6}{1000}=120.$$

**Answer.** $120\,\text{t/day}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Chlorine is applied at $5\,\text{mg/L}$ and the residual is $0.5\,\text{mg/L}$. The chlorine demand is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 190" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="20" x2="38" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="30" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">residual</text><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">Cl₂ dose →</text><path d="M38 165 L95 120 L150 150 L200 158 L300 70" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="200" y1="60" x2="200" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="178" y="55" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">breakpoint</text><text x="95" y="112" font-size="7" fill="#1e293b">combined</text><text x="255" y="80" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">free residual</text></svg>
figure.caption: Breakpoint chlorination: residual vs. applied dose.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Demand = applied − residual.

**Working.**
$$5-0.5=4.5.$$

**Answer.** $4.5$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: Atmospheric CO₂ is $410\,\text{ppm}$ rising at $2\,\text{ppm/yr}$. After $14$ years it is _____ ppm.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Linear extrapolation: C = C₀ + rate×t.

**Working.**
$$410+2\times14=438.$$

**Answer.** $438\,\text{ppm}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Decomposable waste $2500\,\text{kg}$ yields $0.12\,\text{m}^3/\text{kg}$ gas. Total gas is _____ m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><rect x="30" y="40" width="280" height="86" fill="#a16207" opacity="0.5"/><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="10" fill="#65a30d"/><rect x="30" y="126" width="280" height="14" fill="#1e3a8a" opacity="0.6"/><rect x="30" y="140" width="280" height="10" fill="#0f172a"/><text x="170" y="26" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">final cover / vegetation</text><text x="170" y="86" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">compacted MSW + daily cover</text><text x="170" y="137" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">leachate collection</text><text x="170" y="148" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">composite liner (clay+HDPE)</text><path d="M315 60 q14 0 14 14 q0 10 -8 18" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="318" y="56" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">gas vent</text></svg>
figure.caption: Engineered sanitary landfill: cover, waste cell, liner and leachate system.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gas = mass × yield.

**Working.**
$$2500\times0.12=300.$$

**Answer.** $300$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: If $[\text{H}^+]=1e-6\,\text{mol/L}$, the pH is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 90" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><defs><linearGradient id="ph" x1="0" x2="1"><stop offset="0" stop-color="#dc2626"/><stop offset="0.5" stop-color="#16a34a"/><stop offset="1" stop-color="#2563eb"/></linearGradient></defs><rect x="30" y="34" width="260" height="20" fill="url(#ph)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="30" y="68" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0 (acidic)</text><text x="150" y="68" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">7</text><text x="290" y="68" font-size="9" text-anchor="end" fill="#1e293b">14 (basic)</text><polygon points="141.42857142857142,28 136.4,18 146.4,18" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="141.42857142857142" y="14" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">pH ≈ 6</text></svg>
figure.caption: Position on the 0–14 pH scale.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** pH = −log[H⁺].

**Working.**
$$-\log(1e-6)=6.$$

**Answer.** $6$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Read the loose and compacted densities from the bar chart. The volume reduction $(1-\rho_1/\rho_2)$ achieved is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="130.8" width="50" height="19.2" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="126.8" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">80</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">loose ρ₁</text><rect x="180" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">500</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">compacted ρ₂</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kg/m³</text></svg>
figure.caption: Loose and compacted densities shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{80}{500}\right)\times100=84\%.$$

**Answer.** $84\%$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: MPN (Most Probable Number) test in water analysis estimates the density of
questions.solution:
**Concept.** MPN is a statistical estimate of coliform density.

**Working.**
Coliforms.

**Answer.** Coliform bacteria

figure.caption: Present population and carrying capacity shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Utilisation = population/carrying capacity ×100, both read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{77000}{150000}\times100=51.3\%.$$

**Answer.** $51.3\%$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Read the loose and compacted densities from the bar chart. The volume reduction $(1-\rho_1/\rho_2)$ achieved is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="126" width="50" height="24" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="122" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">100</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">loose ρ₁</text><rect x="180" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">500</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">compacted ρ₂</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kg/m³</text></svg>
figure.caption: Loose and compacted densities shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{100}{500}\right)\times100=80\%.$$

**Answer.** $80\%$

questions.stem: $500$ cells after $4$ generations of binary fission number _____.

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: Atmospheric CO₂ is $420\,\text{ppm}$ rising at $2\,\text{ppm/yr}$. After $10$ years it is _____ ppm.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Linear extrapolation: C = C₀ + rate×t.

**Working.**
$$420+2\times10=440.$$

**Answer.** $440\,\text{ppm}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $5\,\text{t}$ of methane (GWP$_{100}=25$). Its carbon-dioxide-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 125</text><rect x="190" y="145.2" width="46" height="4.8" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="141.2" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 5</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CH₄ → CO₂-e (t)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Methane converted to CO₂-equivalent via GWP.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e $=$ mass $\times$ GWP.

**Working.**
$$5\times25=125.$$

**Answer.** $125\,\text{t CO}_2\text{-e}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: For the chimney shown, the physical stack height $h_s$ and plume rise $\Delta h$ are marked on the diagram. The effective stack height is _____ m.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 220" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="185" x2="300" y2="185" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="60" y="85" width="16" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><line x1="68" y1="85" x2="68" y2="42" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.2" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="68" cy="42" r="3" fill="#2563eb"/><line x1="40" y1="185" x2="40" y2="85" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1"/><text x="6" y="138" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">h_s = 100 m</text><line x1="92" y1="85" x2="92" y2="42" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1"/><text x="98" y="66" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">Δh = 20 m</text><text x="150" y="36" font-size="9" fill="#2563eb">plume centreline</text></svg>
figure.caption: Physical stack height h_s and plume rise Δh marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** H = h_s + Δh, with both read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$100+20=120.$$

**Answer.** $120\,\text{m}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays first-order with $k=0.1\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Reading the initial concentration $C_0$ from the figure, its concentration after $8$ d is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 170" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="120" y="75" width="60" height="75" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.25" stroke="#1e293b"/><rect x="120" y="30" width="60" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t = 0</text><text x="150" y="96" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">C₀ = 150</text><text x="150" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial concentration C₀ marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = C₀e^{−kt}, with C₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$150e^{-0.1\times8}=67.4.$$

**Answer.** $67.4$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: The main purpose of maintaining ~850–1100 °C with adequate residence time in an MSW incinerator is to
options: ensure complete combustion and destroy dioxins/pathogens
reduce the need for air pollution control
questions.solution:
**Concept.** High temperature + 2 s residence ensures complete burnout and dioxin destruction.

**Working.**
Complete combustion.

**Answer.** Complete combustion

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=100\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.4</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 40</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.4\times100}{40+100}=0.286.$$

**Answer.** $0.286$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $8\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹1,20,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 2</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{120000}{(1+0.08)^{2}}=102881.$$

**Answer.** $₹102881$

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Σ massᵢ × GWPᵢ, with the three masses read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$80+2\times28+0.5\times265=268.5.$$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 500 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 2 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle settles the marked depth in the marked time; v_s = depth ÷ time. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $v_s=\text{depth}\div\text{time}$, both read from the trajectory.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{2\,\text{m}}{500\,\text{s}}\times1000=4\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $4\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: Read the Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations from the bar chart. The total hardness as CaCO₃ (eq. wt Ca 20, Mg 12.15) is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">80</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Ca²⁺</text><rect x="180" y="123" width="50" height="27" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="119" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">18</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Mg²⁺</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Convert each ion to CaCO₃ (×50/eq.wt) and sum, with both concentrations read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$80\times\dfrac{50}{20}+18\times\dfrac{50}{12.15}=274.1.$$

**Answer.** $274.1$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $Q=80\,\text{g/s}$. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level centreline concentration (full reflection) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 50 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 6 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 30 m,  σ_z = 15 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = Q/(πuσ_yσ_z)·exp(−H²/2σ_z²); H,u,σ_y,σ_z read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{80}{\pi\times6\times30\times15}e^{-50^2/(2\times15^2)}=0.00003646.$$

**Answer.** $0.365\times10^{-4}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A water contains $60\,\text{mg/L}$ of $\text{Ca}^{2+}$ and $24\,\text{mg/L}$ of $\text{Mg}^{2+}$. The total hardness as $\text{CaCO}_3$ is _____ mg/L. (Eq. wt: Ca 20, Mg 12.15, CaCO₃ 50)

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="18" x2="38" y2="160" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="160" x2="305" y2="160" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="14" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="240" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant added →</text><line x1="38" y1="89" x2="305" y2="89" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 3"/><text x="42" y="86" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">pH 7</text><path d="M38 150 C120 146 160 140 168 89 C176 38 230 32 305 28" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="168" cy="89" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence pt</text></svg>
figure.caption: EDTA titration is used to measure total hardness.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Convert each ion to $\text{CaCO}_3$: $\text{mg/L}\times\dfrac{50}{\text{eq.wt}}$.

**Working.**
$$60\times\dfrac{50}{20}+24\times\dfrac{50}{12.15}=248.8.$$

**Answer.** $248.8\,\text{mg/L as CaCO}_3$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: When the environmental lapse rate is greater than the dry adiabatic lapse rate, the atmosphere is

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="10" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">altitude</text><text x="245" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">temperature →</text><line x1="120" y1="160" x2="220" y2="30" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3" stroke-dasharray="5 3"/><text x="222" y="34" font-size="8" fill="#475569">DALR (dry adiabatic)</text><line x1="150" y1="160" x2="200" y2="30" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.6"/><text x="172" y="50" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">ELR (unstable)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Environmental lapse rate vs. dry adiabatic lapse rate — unstable atmosphere.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** ELR > DALR ⇒ a displaced parcel keeps rising ⇒ superadiabatic, unstable (good dispersion, looping plume).

**Working.**
Superadiabatic, unstable.

**Answer.** Superadiabatic / unstable

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Decomposable waste $1000\,\text{kg}$ yields $0.2\,\text{m}^3/\text{kg}$ gas. Total gas is _____ m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><rect x="30" y="40" width="280" height="86" fill="#a16207" opacity="0.5"/><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="10" fill="#65a30d"/><rect x="30" y="126" width="280" height="14" fill="#1e3a8a" opacity="0.6"/><rect x="30" y="140" width="280" height="10" fill="#0f172a"/><text x="170" y="26" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">final cover / vegetation</text><text x="170" y="86" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">compacted MSW + daily cover</text><text x="170" y="137" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">leachate collection</text><text x="170" y="148" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">composite liner (clay+HDPE)</text><path d="M315 60 q14 0 14 14 q0 10 -8 18" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="318" y="56" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">gas vent</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gas = mass × yield.

**Working.**
$$1000\times0.2=200.$$

**Answer.** $200$

figure.caption: Energy content of the waste fuel.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the modified Dulong formula.

**Working.**
$$337(40)+1419(6-40/8)=14899.$$

**Answer.** $14899\,\text{kJ/kg}$

questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.02\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $9$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 2000</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 40 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 4 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 40 m,  σ_z = 25 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>

**Concept.** $C=\dfrac{Q}{\pi u\sigma_y\sigma_z}\exp\!\left(-\dfrac{H^2}{2\sigma_z^2}\right)$ (ground, centreline, full reflection); $H,u,\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{80}{\pi\times4\times40\times25}e^{-40^2/(2\times25^2)}=0.00177004\,\text{g/m}^3.$$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $Q=80\,\text{g/s}$. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level centreline concentration (full reflection) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 50 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 4 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 25 m,  σ_z = 20 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = Q/(πuσ_yσ_z)·exp(−H²/2σ_z²); H,u,σ_y,σ_z read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{80}{\pi\times4\times25\times20}e^{-50^2/(2\times20^2)}=0.00055942.$$

**Answer.** $5.594\times10^{-4}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A facility emits CO₂, CH₄ (GWP 28) and N₂O (GWP 265); the three masses (t) are shown on the bar chart. The total carbon footprint is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 188" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="36" y1="150" x2="304" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="60" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="83" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">100</text><text x="83" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂</text><rect x="142" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="165" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">2</text><text x="165" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CH₄</text><rect x="224" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="247" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">2</text><text x="247" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">N₂O</text><text x="170" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: Masses of CO₂, CH₄ and N₂O shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Σ massᵢ × GWPᵢ, with the three masses read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$100+2\times28+2\times265=686.$$

**Answer.** $686$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: A river has DO saturation $9\,\text{mg/L}$. From the oxygen-sag curve shown, read the critical deficit $D_c$. The minimum dissolved oxygen in the stream is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 200" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><line x1="34" y1="30" x2="34" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="34" y1="175" x2="330" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="8" y="40" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">DO</text><text x="305" y="190" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">distance/time →</text><line x1="34" y1="45" x2="330" y2="45" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="250" y="42" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">saturation DO</text><path d="M34 60 Q120 175 175 150 Q260 110 330 70" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="175" y1="45" x2="175" y2="150" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="175" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="180" y="120" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">D_c = 2.5 mg/L</text><text x="150" y="170" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">t_c = 1.5 d</text></svg>
figure.caption: Critical deficit D_c (and t_c) marked on the sag curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Minimum DO = DO_sat − D_c, with D_c read from the sag curve.

**Working.**
$$9-2.5=6.5.$$

**Answer.** $6.5\,\text{mg/L}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.08\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $11$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 1000</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial population N₀ marked on the curve at t = 0. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$1000e^{0.08\times11}=2411.$$

**Answer.** $2411$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: In the energy pyramid shown, only the producer (trophic-1) energy is marked; 10% transfers per level. Energy at trophic level $3$ is _____ kcal/m²/yr.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 180" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><polygon points="40,150 280,150 240,118 80,118" fill="#16a34a" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="80,118 240,118 205,86 115,86" fill="#65a30d" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="115,86 205,86 178,54 142,54" fill="#d97706" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="142,54 178,54 165,30 155,30" fill="#dc2626" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="160" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">Producers = 8000</text><text x="160" y="110" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L2 = ?</text><text x="160" y="78" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L3 = ?</text><text x="160" y="46" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L4 = ?</text><text x="160" y="172" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kcal/m²/yr — 10% transfer per level</text></svg>
figure.caption: Only the producer (trophic level 1) energy is marked; upper levels follow the 10% rule. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Eₙ = E₀(0.1)ⁿ, with E₀ read from the pyramid.

**Working.**
$$8000\times0.1^{2}=80.$$

**Answer.** $80$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: $50\,\text{mL}$ of an acid is titrated with $0.1\,\text{N}$ NaOH. Reading the equivalence-point volume $V_{eq}$ from the titration curve shown, the normality of the acid is _____ N.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 195" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="16" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="220" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant volume (mL) →</text><path d="M40 150 C120 146 160 140 168 92 C176 44 230 36 305 30" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="168" y1="30" x2="168" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="168" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence</text><text x="158" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">25</text></svg>
figure.caption: Equivalence-point titrant volume marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At the equivalence point $N_aV_a=N_bV_b$, with $V_{eq}$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$N_a=\dfrac{0.1\times25}{50}=0.05.$$

**Answer.** $0.05\,\text{N}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $6\,\text{t}$ of a greenhouse gas. Reading the gas's GWP₁₀₀ from the figure, its CO₂-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="36" width="220" height="56" rx="6" fill="rgba(217,119,6,0.12)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">the gas</text><text x="150" y="80" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">GWP₁₀₀ = 25</text><text x="150" y="112" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂-e = mass × GWP</text></svg>
figure.caption: Global-warming potential of the gas marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$6\times25=150.$$

**Answer.** $150$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: $25\,\text{mL}$ of an acid is titrated with $0.1\,\text{N}$ NaOH. Reading the equivalence-point volume $V_{eq}$ from the titration curve shown, the normality of the acid is _____ N.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 195" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="16" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="220" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant volume (mL) →</text><path d="M40 150 C120 146 160 140 168 92 C176 44 230 36 305 30" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="168" y1="30" x2="168" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="168" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence</text><text x="158" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">20</text></svg>
figure.caption: Equivalence-point titrant volume marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At the equivalence point $N_aV_a=N_bV_b$, with $V_{eq}$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$N_a=\dfrac{0.1\times20}{25}=0.08.$$

**Answer.** $0.08\,\text{N}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: At a receptor, $3$ identical machines each produce $85\,\text{dB}$. The combined sound pressure level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $L=L_1+10\log_{10}n$ for $n$ equal incoherent sources.

**Working.**
$$85+10\log_{10}3=89.77.$$

**Answer.** $89.77\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: For the landfill shown, read the usable air-space and the annual fill rate from the diagram. Its design life is _____ years.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><rect x="30" y="40" width="280" height="100" fill="#a16207" opacity="0.45"/><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="10" fill="#65a30d"/><text x="170" y="86" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">air-space = 3,00,000 m³</text><text x="170" y="108" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">fill rate = 50,000 m³/yr</text><text x="170" y="166" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">design life = air-space ÷ fill rate</text></svg>
figure.caption: Landfill air-space and annual fill rate marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life = air-space/annual fill, both read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{300000}{50000}=6.$$

**Answer.** $6\,\text{yr}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: Read the Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations from the bar chart. The total hardness as CaCO₃ (eq. wt Ca 20, Mg 12.15) is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">80</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Ca²⁺</text><rect x="180" y="114" width="50" height="36" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="110" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">24</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Mg²⁺</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Convert each ion to CaCO₃ (×50/eq.wt) and sum, with both concentrations read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$80\times\dfrac{50}{20}+24\times\dfrac{50}{12.15}=298.8.$$

**Answer.** $298.8$
